relevant_facts: Buffered Event Timing entries use the default 104 ms threshold.
A custom durationThreshold applies to future observations and is clamped to a minimum of 16 ms.
interactionId groups related event entries, while interactionCount counts distinct interactions.

finding: Absence of buffered or threshold-filtered Event Timing entries is generally partial evidence, not proof that no interaction occurred or that responsiveness was good.
design_implication: Replay and responsiveness systems must carry observer mode, threshold, observer start completeness, interaction-count availability, and count relationships before interpreting missing entries.

verification.environment: isolated local Node.js runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The Event Timing specification is a Working Draft and may change.
Browser support and delivery behavior were not measured on a physical iPhone in this run.
A complete observation window does not prove every meaningful user action produces an Event Timing interaction.
falsification_route: Reject the gate if a conforming implementation permits live durationThreshold values below 16 ms.
Reject the absence rule if buffered retrieval is standardized to include all sub-104 ms event entries.
Use physical browser packets to test whether the declared observer window and interactionCount behavior match runtime observations.
privacy_boundary: No target selectors, DOM text, private chat, health material, credentials, or identifying user data are stored.
